mnemonics.title: PUE Formula Memory
mnemonics.content: PUE = Total Facility Energy ÷ IT Equipment Energy. Remember: 'People Usually Eat' - People (P) = PUE, Usually = Usage, Eat = Energy. The IT energy goes in the denominator (bottom).

mnemonics.title: GHG Protocol Scopes
mnemonics.content: Scope 1 = Direct (from your own sources like vehicles), Scope 2 = Energy间接 (electricity you buy), Scope 3 = Everything Else (supply chain, travel). Remember: 1-Direct, 2-Energy Purchase, 3-Third party/Value chain.

mnemonics.title: Green IT Metrics
mnemonics.content: PUE = Power, CUE = Carbon, WUE = Water, EE = Energy Efficiency. All formulas relate output to input - effectiveness = Output/Input.

acronyms.usage: Data center energy efficiency measurement (Total Facility Energy / IT Energy)
Carbon emissions per unit of energy (kgCO2e / kWh)
Gases contributing to climate change (CO2, CH4, N2O, etc.)
Method to evaluate environmental impacts from cradle to grave
CO2 emitted per unit of electricity (kgCO2/kWh)
Industry consortium for data center energy efficiency standards
Three central factors in measuring sustainability and ethical impact

visualTips.visualization: Picture a pie chart: The entire circle is Total Facility Energy. The IT Equipment slice shows energy actually doing useful work. The remaining slice is overhead (cooling, lighting). Divide IT slice by total to get PUE - smaller overhead = better PUE.

Think of three concentric circles: Scope 1 is the smallest inner circle (direct control), Scope 2 surrounds it (energy you purchase), Scope 3 is the largest outer ring extending to your entire value chain/supply chain.
Visualize a scale: On one side, all ICT activities (data centers, devices, networks). On the other side, the weight of CO2 emissions they produce. The heavier the CO2 side, the larger the footprint.

Draw a diagonal line from bottom-left to top-right. The x-axis is workload (0-100%), y-axis is power consumption. Perfect proportionality means power scales exactly with workload. Real servers curve above this line.

analogies.analogy: PUE is like fuel efficiency in a car. Just as MPG shows how efficiently fuel converts to miles, PUE shows how efficiently electricity converts to computing work. Lower PUE = better 'fuel economy'.

E-waste is like toxic waste from chemical plants but from our digital devices. Just as chemical waste needs special handling, old phones and computers contain harmful materials requiring specialized recycling.

Virtualization is like a building with multiple apartments. One physical building (server) houses many separate living spaces (virtual machines), making much better use of the available space and resources.

Scope 3 emissions are like the environmental impact of a restaurant's entire supply chain - from farm where vegetables grow, to transport trucks, to the disposable containers used for takeout - everything beyond their direct kitchen operations.
